analysis.title: Interaction report
analysis.empty: Select Drug A and Drug B, then run analysis. Results use rule-based assessment with FDA label search — no cloud AI required.
analysis.aiLoading: AI is assessing this drug pair…

search.label: FDA label search
search.typeToSearch: Keep typing — local aliases appear instantly; FDA results follow.

analyze.hint: Assign both Drug A and Drug B to analyze.
analyze.readyHint: Both slots filled — run rule-based interaction analysis.
analyze.aiReadyHint: Both slots filled — AI will assess this pair directly (no FDA rule lookup).
analyze.doneHint: Analysis complete — run again after changing drugs.
analyze.aiDoneHint: Assistant responded — ask a follow-up or run again to refresh.

inputRail.drugExplainEmpty: Assign a drug to this slot first.
inputRail.drugExplainFailed: Could not generate a drug explanation.

reportEmpty.fallback: Select drugs and run analysis to populate this section.

noPair.emergency: Emergency signs appear here after you analyze a drug pair.
noPair.summary: Select Drug A and B, then run analysis for a pair summary.
noPair.mechanism: Mechanism details will appear once both drugs are assigned and analyzed.
noPair.expectedEffects: Expected effects will list here after analysis.
noPair.practicalSteps: Practical steps will appear after you run interaction analysis.

awaitAnalysis.emergency: Run analysis to see when to seek emergency care for this pair.
awaitAnalysis.summary: Press Analyze interaction to generate the pair summary.
awaitAnalysis.mechanism: Mechanism analysis loads when you run interaction analysis.
awaitAnalysis.expectedEffects: Expected effects populate after analysis completes.
awaitAnalysis.practicalSteps: Practical steps appear after you analyze this pair.

report.translationFailedHint: Could not translate this report. Check server AI keys in .env or add a provider key in Settings → AI, then run analysis again.
report.aiFailedHint: Could not generate an AI interaction report. Check your provider key in Settings → AI or server AI keys in .env, then try again.
report.aiNotConfiguredHint: Assistant-first mode needs an AI provider. Add a key in Settings → AI or configure server AI keys, then run analysis again.

inspector.noAnalysis: Run analysis to populate session metrics.
inspector.sourceNote: Drug names from FDA OpenFDA label API via Biolabs server proxy. Interaction assessment is rule-based (local).

guide.title: Getting started
guide.step1: Select Drug A or B in Input, then search FDA labels.
guide.step2: Confirm each search result before assigning to a slot.
guide.step3: Run interaction analysis to populate the report and session metrics.

drugProfile.title: About this drug
drugProfile.selectHint: Pick a slot in Input, search FDA labels, or tap Drug A / B below to inspect.
drugProfile.noOverview: No structured label text is available yet. Try a generic name or check FDA search spelling.

layout.binaryEmpty: Select two drugs and run analysis.
layout.commandAwaitAnalysis: Run analysis to populate these panels.

welcome.title: Binary
welcome.body: I assess drug–drug interactions for you. Search two drugs on the left, then ask me to analyze — or type what you need in the prompt below.
welcome.suggestion1: Search Drug A and Drug B, then run analysis
welcome.suggestion2: Ask: "Find warfarin and aspirin and analyze the interaction"
welcome.suggestion3: Educational use only — not medical advice

assistantFirst.historyEmpty: Recent FDA searches appear here after you assign drugs.
assistantFirst.inlineDisclaimer: Educational synthesis — not verified FDA label text unless cited.
assistantFirst.dataSource: Data: FDA OpenFDA labels via Biolabs proxy · AI-generated assessment

disclaimer: For educational use only. Not medical advice. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional before changing medications.

assistant.notConfiguredTitle: AI not configured — add a provider key in Settings
assistant.primaryEmpty: Assign Drug A and B, then run Analyze — the assistant will assess this pair directly in chat.
assistant.primaryFollowUp: Ask a follow-up about this pair, or run Analyze again to refresh the assessment.
assistant.analyzePairPrompt: Analyze the drug-drug interaction between {{drugA}} and {{drugB}}. Assess risk level, mechanism, expected effects, what to monitor, practical steps, and emergency warning signs. Be concise but complete. Educational use only.
assistant.selectPairHint: Pick Drug A and B — quick prompts and context appear here.
assistant.runAnalysisHint: Run interaction analysis to load rule-based context.
assistant.aiRunAnalysisHint: Ask about this pair — the AI report panel fills when analysis completes.
assistant.fusedFollowUp: Ask a follow-up about the report above, or use quick prompts.
assistant.notConfigured: Add an AI provider in Settings to use chat and quick prompts.
assistant.placeholder: Ask about this drug pair, monitoring, or alternatives…
assistant.emptyWithPair: Use quick prompts above or ask a follow-up about this pair.
assistant.dismissPairContext: Remove drug pair from this chat
assistant.autoReviewIntro: Review this Phaeleon drug interaction assessment. Keep the response concise and practical.

prompts.explainRisk: Explain the interaction risk between {{drugA}} and {{drugB}}. Current assessment: {{risk}}.
prompts.clinicalSummary: Give a concise clinical summary for co-administration of {{drugA}} and {{drugB}}.
prompts.monitoring: What should be monitored when {{drugA}} and {{drugB}} are taken together?
prompts.alternatives: What safer alternatives or timing adjustments should a clinician discuss for {{drugA}} with {{drugB}}?
prompts.emergencySigns: What emergency warning signs should someone watch for when taking {{drugA}} with {{drugB}}?

sectionLinks.emergencyPrompt: Summarize emergency warning signs for {{drugA}} + {{drugB}}. Reference: {{anchor}}
